title: Driver Fatigue Detection (Trans Track)
subtitle: Industrial collaboration with Bandung Techno Park and Trans Track: real-time fatigue detection for commercial fleet dashcams
reflection: MultiscaleTCN had to outperform a model the company was already running in production. That is a different kind of pressure than a benchmark.
Real industrial data from mining fleets is messier and more unbalanced than anything I had cleaned before. The real world does not come labeled.
I learned the difference between a demo and a service. A demo runs once. A service runs at 3am when a driver is falling asleep on a highway.
39 tests, all running against the live MDVR server. I learned to write tests for real hardware, not mock objects.
The night-hour heuristic and the driver history rule came from real incidents. Edge cases in safety systems are not edge cases. They are the whole point.

highlights: Industry project with Trans Track, real dashcam footage from mining company fleets (BIB, PAMA MTBU, SIS). Not synthetic data.
Built the dataset downloader first: pulled alarm events and video clips directly from the TransTrack platform.
Designed MultiscaleTCN from scratch: 3 parallel TCN branches at different dilation scales (fine [1,2,4,8], medium [2,4,8,16], coarse [4,8,16,32]) running on 200-frame sequences of facial landmarks.
Input: EAR left/right, MAR, head pitch/yaw/roll, nose position. 11 values per frame, extracted by MediaPipe at 10 FPS.
Three classes: eyes closed, normal, yawning. Test accuracy 0.85, macro F1 0.78. Baseline was 0.67. That is an 11% gain on a model the company already had in production.
FastAPI service: HTTP 202 immediately, full pipeline async. Night-hour heuristic fires 00:00-05:59 when confidence drops below 0.75. Driver history rule escalates on 2+ fatigue events in 60 minutes.
3-service Docker stack. 39 tests passing against the live MDVR server.
